page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-64 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, providing two threads per core. The system is equipped with 256 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations are highly efficient, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets, as reflected in strong Passmark CPU encryption and Redis SET operation benchmarks. Memory performance is mixed; small-block cached read bandwidth is highly competitive, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier.

While the instance delivers strong performance for multi-threaded tasks such as ray tracing, image processing, and database operations, it exhibits limitations under heavy memory-bound or large-scale AI workloads. For instance, LLM text generation using a large 70B model falls into the bottom 10% of performance. Given these characteristics, the instance is best suited for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads, enterprise database operations, and medium-scale web serving rather than large-scale LLM inference or storage-heavy applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-64 is a general-purpose server configured with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. Featuring 32 physical cores with hyperthreading, it delivers strong multi-core processing and cryptographic performance, as evidenced by top-tier stress-ng and Passmark CPU encryption benchmarks. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. While cached memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations perform in the top 25%, large-block uncached memory transfers and large LLM text generation tasks show average to poor performance. This resource profile makes the instance highly suitable for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database operations, and general-purpose compute tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-64 is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-64 features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose compute instance featuring sixty-four virtual AMD EPYC cores and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of memory.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 architecture
Strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators included
High-throughput cached memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations
Suited for database operations, enterprise applications, and general-purpose compute
